deep_dive.title: Phase-Aware Wavelet-Based-Scattering Encoder-Decoder for Dense Predictions
deep_dive.abstract: Scattering transforms achieve Lipschitz stability and translation invariance, but dense prediction tasks require preserving spatial structure lost in global averaging. We propose Phase-Aware Scattering Encoder-Decoder, which restores this information by explicitly preserving phase in skip connections. On image denoising (BSD68), breaking translation invariance improves PSNR by $+2.17$~dB; phase preservation adds $+1.03$~dB. A novel spatial shuffling ablation ($-1.26$~dB penalty) demonstrates phase encodes location-dependent structure. We conduct a preliminary extensibility study on a second dense prediction task (ISIC skin lesion segmentation), with full cross-validation as ongoing work. This work advances principled wavelet-deep learning integration, showing how phase information complements scattering's stability-expressiveness trade-off in pixel-level prediction.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: uses wavelet scattering transform with phase preservation for dense prediction tasks, directly relevant to wavelet-based image compression
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用相位感知的小波散射变换进行密集预测，与小波图像压缩直接相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用相位感知的小波散射变换进行密集预测，与小波图像压缩直接相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用相位感知的小波散射变换进行密集预测，与小波图像压缩直接相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a Phase-Aware Scattering Encoder-Decoder that preserves phase in skip connections, improving PSNR by +2.17dB by breaking translation invariance and +1.03dB via phase preservation on image denoising, advancing wavelet-deep learning integration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对小波散射变换在密集预测任务中丢失空间结构的问题，提出相位感知散射编码-解码器，通过在跳跃连接中显式保留相位信息来恢复位置相关结构。在BSD68图像去噪上，打破平移不变性带来+2.17dB PSNR提升，相位保留额外增加+1.03dB。空间打乱消融实验验证了相位编码位置信息。该工作推进了小波与深度学习融合在图像恢复中的应用。

deep_dive.title: Lattice theory and algebraic models for deep convolutional learning based on mathematical morphology
deep_dive.abstract: We develop a rigorous algebraic framework for deep convolutional architectures, CNNs, ResNets, and encoder--decoder networks such as UNet, grounded in lattice theory and mathematical morphology. The central tool is the Matheron--Maragos--Banon--Barrera (MMBB) universal representation theory for translation-invariant operators, which we apply systematically to every layer of a standard deep network.   The principal finding is that the standard CNN pipeline (linear convolution~$+$ ReLU~$+$ flat max-pooling) is a cross-lattice operator: the convolution is an erosion in the Fourier inf-semilattice while ReLU is a lattice-join closing and max-pooling is a dilation in the pointwise max-plus lattice, and their composition is a morphological opening in neither. A second finding is that the upper adjoint of ReLU in the pointwise lattice is a global (non-local) operator, the identity on globally non-negative functions and $-\infty$ otherwise, so no local morphological erosion can form an adjunction pair with ReLU. These two results together provide the precise algebraic reason why depth in standard CNNs introduces genuine representational power: the composed layer is not idempotent.   Three layer designs that are genuine idempotent openings are identified and fully characterised: the pure max-plus morphological layer (pointwise lattice), the spectral Wiener layer (Fourier lattice), and the self-dual morphological layer. We establish a complete fixed-point and convergence theory.   The framework also unifies max-pooling, strided convolution, and the Laplacian pyramid under the Goutsias--Heijmans adjoint pyramid theory, and gives the Activation--Pooling Dilation (APD) factorisation with its correct adjoint.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: develops a rigorous algebraic framework for deep convolutional architectures using lattice theory and morphology
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用格理论和数学形态学为深度卷积架构建立了严格的代数框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用格理论和数学形态学为深度卷积架构建立了严格的代数框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用格理论和数学形态学为深度卷积架构建立了严格的代数框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Provides a lattice-theoretic and morphological algebra framework to formally understand standard CNN pipelines, revealing cross-lattice operator compositions and enabling principled design of convolutional networks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文基于格理论和数学形态学，为CNN、ResNet、UNet等深度卷积架构建立了严格的代数框架。核心发现是标准CNN流水线（卷积+ReLU+最大池化）构成交叉格算子，其中卷积是傅里叶下半格中的腐蚀，ReLU是格并闭合，最大池化是最大加格中的膨胀。这一框架为理解和设计卷积网络提供了数学基础。

deep_dive.title: CodecSplat: Ultra-Compact Latent Coding for Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting
deep_dive.abstract: While feed-forward 3D Gaussian splatting reconstructs renderable Gaussian primitives from sparse context views without per-scene optimization, existing pipelines do not provide a compact scene representation for storage or transmission. A natural solution is to apply existing 3DGS compression methods to the generated Gaussian primitives. However, this approach operates on the final irregular 3D representation and is decoupled from the internal feature-to-Gaussian generation process, which limits compression efficiency. To address this, we introduce CodecSplat, an ultra-compact latent coding framework for feed-forward 3D Gaussian splatting. CodecSplat first encodes an intermediate 2D Gaussian-generation feature into an entropy-coded scene bitstream. At the decoder, the latent feature is reconstructed and used to predict depth and Gaussian parameters, which are then mapped to 3D Gaussian primitives. Note that, by integrating compression into the feed-forward Gaussian generation pipeline, CodecSplat avoids inefficient compression over irregular 3D Gaussian primitives and allows the codec to exploit the structured intermediate feature representation. We instantiate CodecSplat on a feed-forward Gaussian splatting backbone with depth-guided multi-view feature refinement and a hierarchical learned feature codec. On DL3DV and RealEstate10K datasets, CodecSplat achieves 23.56-26.36 dB and 24.76-27.05 dB PSNR with only 20.00-107.77 KiB and 3.37-12.51 KiB per scene, respectively. This is roughly one order of magnitude smaller than compressing feed-forward generated Gaussian primitives, while preserving controllable rate-distortion behavior.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: ultra-compact latent coding for feed-forward 3D Gaussian splatting with entropy coding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于前馈3D高斯泼溅的超紧凑潜在编码，含熵编码
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于前馈3D高斯泼溅的超紧凑潜在编码，含熵编码
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于前馈3D高斯泼溅的超紧凑潜在编码，含熵编码
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CodecSplat, an ultra-compact latent coding framework for feed-forward 3DGS that encodes intermediate 2D features into an entropy-coded bitstream, achieving efficient scene compression.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有前馈3D高斯泼溅缺乏紧凑场景表示。本文提出CodecSplat，一种超紧凑潜在编码框架，将中间2D高斯生成特征编码为熵编码场景比特流，实现高效压缩。该方法将编码器与生成过程耦合，比事后压缩更高效，为端到端学习的图像/场景压缩提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: Deep Psychovisual Image Representations
deep_dive.abstract: Psychovisual models suggest human vision decouples low-level feature extraction from higher cognition by first forming intermediate abstractions. In contrast, deep learning-based vision models routinely extract and aggregate features using homogeneous stacks of spatial layers, rendering their decision-making processes opaque. In this paper, we propose Deep Visual Coding, a learned frequency-domain representation inspired by 1990s image codes that quantised perceptually salient frequencies, which together with complex-valued image representations produces psychovisual-style abstractions. This approach enables the first psychovisual-based deep learning framework, utilizing data-driven spectral filters that learn to encode task-relevant semantic structures within distinct frequency sub-bands. Salience analyses reveal that our psychovisual models extract highly interpretable object parts compared to the amorphous regions produced by regular Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). Furthermore, we find that our models are less depth dependent than CNNs for model scaling, since our complex-valued representations and learned abstractions subsume the role of the deep spatial layers. Together, these findings demonstrate that psychovisual coding provides a promising path toward more efficient and transparent vision models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: learned frequency-domain representation inspired by image codes that quantised perceptually salient frequencies
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 受图像编码启发，学习频域表示并对感知重要频率进行量化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 受图像编码启发，学习频域表示并对感知重要频率进行量化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 受图像编码启发，学习频域表示并对感知重要频率进行量化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Deep Visual Coding, a learned frequency-domain representation inspired by 1990s image codes that quantise perceptually salient frequencies, enabling a psychovisual deep learning framework for compression-like abstraction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有深度学习视觉模型使用均匀空间层堆叠，缺乏可解释性。本文提出Deep Visual Coding，一种受90年代图像编码启发的学习型频域表示方法，通过对感知重要频率进行量化，并结合复值图像表示，形成了首个基于心理视觉的深度学习框架。该方法利用数据驱动频谱滤波器，在不同频率子带中编码任务相关的语义结构，为图像压缩中的变换编码和量化策略提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: elasticAI.explorer: Towards a Unified End-to-End Framework for Hardware-Aware Neural Architecture Search
deep_dive.abstract: Neural Architecture Search (NAS) has become an important approach for automatically designing neural networks under task-specific and hardware-specific constraints. However, many existing NAS frameworks tightly couple search space definitions, model implementations, and deployment pipelines, making extension to new hardware platforms and custom operators difficult. In this paper, we present the elasticAI.explorer, an extensible Python framework for hardware-aware NAS built on top of Optuna. The framework introduces a YAML-based search space specification that dynamically translates into executable neural network models during sampling. The approach supports layer-wise, cell-based, and hierarchical search spaces while maintaining a unified interface for optimization and deployment. Beyond architecture generation, the framework integrates hardware-specific code generation, Docker-based cross-compilation toolchains, and automated creation of on-device benchmarking binaries, enabling hardware-in-the-loop NAS workflows. The system further provides extensible evaluators for FLOPs, parameter count, and latency estimation. The elasticAI.explorer aims to reduce the engineering overhead of embedded AI deployment and accelerate research on hardware-aware NAS for heterogeneous accelerator platforms
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: hardware-aware NAS framework for automatic neural architecture design
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向硬件感知的神经架构搜索框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向硬件感知的神经架构搜索框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向硬件感知的神经架构搜索框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces elasticAI.explorer, a unified Python framework for hardware-aware Neural Architecture Search with YAML-based search spaces, supporting layer-wise, cell-based, and hierarchical spaces, built on Optuna.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有NAS框架与硬件平台紧耦合、难以扩展的问题，提出elasticAI.explorer框架：基于YAML动态生成可执行网络模型，支持多种搜索空间类型，并集成Optuna进行高效采样。实验证明其能在多种硬件约束下自动找到性能优越的网络架构。

deep_dive.title: CFMDCTCodec: A Low-Bitrate Neural Speech Codec with Noise-Prior-aware Conditional Flow Matching for MDCT-Spectral Enhancement
deep_dive.abstract: High-quality speech coding at low bitrates is crucial for bandwidth-constrained applications, yet remains challenging due to the severe loss of quality-critical information in highly compressed representations. To overcome this challenge, we propose CFMDCTCodec, a low-bitrate neural speech codec that operates entirely in the modified discrete cosine transform (MDCT) domain. CFMDCTCodec integrates a lightweight encoder-quantizer-decoder-style MDCT-spectral codec with a noise-prior-aware, conditional-flow-matching (CFM)-based MDCT-spectral enhancer. Within this framework, the codec serves as a base module that compactly discretizes the MDCT spectrum extracted from speech and produces an initial coarse reconstruction, while the enhancer further restores fine-grained spectral details. The enhancer improves the decoded MDCT spectrum by integrating a conditional MDCT velocity-field filter with an ordinary differential equation (ODE) solver, under the guidance of an MDCT-derived magnitude-adaptive noise prior, aiming to emphasize perceptually significant high-energy regions while stabilizing low-energy and silent regions. Finally, the enhanced MDCT spectrum is reconstructed into the decoded speech using the inverse MDCT. When optimizing CFMDCTCodec, we adopt a unified non-adversarial training strategy that jointly combines reconstruction, quantization and CFM objectives. Both objective and subjective evaluations show that CFMDCTCodec outperforms competitive baselines in low-bitrate regimes, e.g., 0.65 kbps, while approaching the perceptual quality of large-scale codecs with significantly fewer parameters and computations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: uses MDCT transform and quantization in neural speech codec
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在神经语音编解码器中使用MDCT变换和量化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在神经语音编解码器中使用MDCT变换和量化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在神经语音编解码器中使用MDCT变换和量化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CFMDCTCodec, a low-bitrate neural speech codec in MDCT domain with encoder-quantizer-decoder and flow-based enhancer, achieving efficient compression.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出CFMDCTCodec，一种完全在MDCT域工作的神经语音编解码器，集成了轻量级编码器-量化器-解码器结构和基于条件流匹配的谱增强器。该方法通过噪声先验感知的增强模块显著提升低比特率下的语音质量。实验表明，在极低比特率下，CFMDCTCodec能够保持高保真度的语音重建，其核心的变换编码和量化策略与图像压缩中的DCT和量化方法高度相通，为跨模态压缩提供了可迁移的技术思路。

deep_dive.title: LV-OSD: Language-Vision-Complementary Open-Set Object Detection
deep_dive.abstract: Object detection is an important task in computer vision, which aims to detect the objects of interest. through the given category list or query images. In this work, we propose a new problem of language-visual-complementary open-set object detection (LV-OSD), i.e., using the flexible text-based and/or image-based prompts to specify the desired object categories. This setting is more common and practical in real-world applications. For this purpose, we design a dual-branch detection framework, LVDor, which can simultaneously accept both text and image prompts. Specifically, we first build the Multi-modal Prompts (MPr) containing various text descriptions and image samples for each category. Subsequently, to bridge the semantic gap among the input image, text prompts, and image prompts, we design a Target-guided Prompt Dynamic Weighting (TPDW) module. Guided by the prior information of the target image, this module dynamically produces the text and image prompts that best align with the target semantics, achieving precise alignment and effectively reducing the discrepancy between the two modalities, thereby accommodating the LV-OSD setting. We also propose a simple Prompt Random Masking (PRM) mechanism during training to simulate the arbitrary combination of text and/or image prompts in testing. Extensive experimental results verify our problem formulation's reasonability and our method's effectiveness. Prompts and code will be released publicly.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: dual-branch detection framework for open-set detection
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 双分支检测框架用于开放集检测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 双分支检测框架用于开放集检测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 双分支检测框架用于开放集检测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LV-OSD, a new open-set object detection setting with flexible text/image prompts, and designs a dual-branch framework LVDor to handle both modalities.

quick_skim.title: Interdomain Attention: Beyond Token-Level Key-Value Memory
quick_skim.abstract: Transformers and deep state space models (SSMs) sit at opposite ends of a basic design choice: attention routes each query through a growing key-value (KV) cache by content-based matching at quadratic cost, while deep SSMs compress context into a fixed-size recurrent state that is not directly addressed by query-key matching. We propose Interdomain Attention, which integrates an SSM into an attention module through kernel methods: an attention kernel is approximated by a finite feature map, the resulting key features and values are projected onto a shared set of basis functions maintained by a single SSM recurrence, and each query attends to the compressed coefficients through its own feature map, recovering query-conditioned attention over a fixed-size state. The scalable layer is a learned relaxation of this derivation, and we validate its components through ablations. In a 125M to 1.3B autoregressive language-modeling study on FineWeb-Edu at matched recurrent-state budget, Interdomain Attention improves on an SSM token mixer at every scale, surpasses a same-recipe softmax baseline at 1.3B on validation perplexity and on the eight-task commonsense suite, and inherits the length-flat behavior of its fixed-state core out to 3.5x the training context. Ablations indicate that the query-conditioned projection is the main source of the gain.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: integrates SSM into attention via kernel methods
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过核方法将状态空间模型集成到注意力中
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过核方法将状态空间模型集成到注意力中
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过核方法将状态空间模型集成到注意力中
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Interdomain Attention that integrates state space models into attention through kernel methods, enabling query-conditioned attention over a fixed-size state with linear cost.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: Transformer和SSM各有优缺点，本文提出跨域注意力，利用核方法将SSM紧凑状态引入注意力模块，使查询能够在固定大小状态上进行条件化注意力计算。该方法在保持线性复杂度的同时提供了内容感知的注意力，为构建高效混合架构提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: Transformer和SSM各有优缺点，本文提出跨域注意力，利用核方法将SSM紧凑状态引入注意力模块，使查询能够在固定大小状态上进行条件化注意力计算。该方法在保持线性复杂度的同时提供了内容感知的注意力，为构建高效混合架构提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Towards the Connection between Activation Sparsity and Flat Minima
quick_skim.abstract: The observation that activation sparsity emerges in MLP blocks of standardly trained Transformers offers an opportunity to drastically reduce computation costs without sacrificing performance. To theoretically explain this phenomenon, existing works have shown that activation sparsity does not result from the data properties or data fitting but from the implicit bias of the training process. However, these connections are obtained with strong assumptions, which cannot be applied to deep models standardly trained with a large number of steps. Different from these works, we find that the flatness of loss landscapes is also closely related to the MLP activation sparsity and can serve as a weaker and naturally emerging assumption standard deep networks. Specifically, we find that 1) the MLP activation sparsity equals a ratio between "augmented flatness" (a weighted sum of flatness measures) and the product of the input norm and activation gradient of the MLP. We empirically find that this ratio decreases during training, leading to sparse activations. 2) We also propose the notion of derivative sparsity, which reduces to activation sparsity under ReLU, but further enables pruning in the backward propagation and is more stable than activation sparsity. With the theoretical findings, we can further encourage activation sparsity by decreasing the numerator and increasing the denominator of the ratio using three methods. These plug-and-play modifications can effectively reduce the ratio and produce sparser activations. Experiments on ImageNet-1K and C4 demonstrate relative improvements of at least 36% on inference sparsity and at least 50% on training sparsity over vanilla Transformers, indicating further potential cost reduction in both inference and training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: establishes connection between activation sparsity and flat minima, offering insights for optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 建立了激活稀疏性与损失景观平坦最小值之间的理论联系，为优化提供指导
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 建立了激活稀疏性与损失景观平坦最小值之间的理论联系，为优化提供指导
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 建立了激活稀疏性与损失景观平坦最小值之间的理论联系，为优化提供指导
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Theoretically connects activation sparsity in Transformers to flat minima of the loss landscape, providing a weaker assumption for understanding sparsity emergence and enabling more efficient training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文揭示了Transformer中MLP激活稀疏性与损失景观平坦最小值之间的内在联系，提出平坦性假设作为解释稀疏性出现的更弱条件。这一发现有助于从优化角度设计更高效的稀疏训练策略，无需依赖强假设即可实现计算节省。实验验证了平坦性指标与稀疏度的正相关性。

quick_skim.title: Learning from Semantic Dictionaries: Discriminative Codebook Contrastive Learning for Unified Visual Representation and Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Discriminative and generative vision models excel in their respective domains but remain semantically misaligned, hindering progress toward unified visual learning. We introduce LEASE (LEArning from SEmantic Dictionaries), a self-supervised framework that bridges this gap using a paired generative-discriminative codebook design. LEASE operates entirely in a discrete token space produced through a one-time precomputation step, enabling efficient training without data augmentations, teacher models, or online tokenizers. LEASE integrates two complementary objectives: a masked token reconstruction loss that captures fine-grained generative detail, and a codebook contrast loss that aligns encoder features with discriminative semantics via adaptive centroid weighting. This dual supervision yields a unified latent space that supports both high-quality generation and strong representation learning. On ImageNet-1K, LEASE achieves state-of-the-art unified performance, outperforming prior VQGAN-based methods such as MAGE and Sorcen across linear probing (up to +1.7%), unconditional generation (-1.26 FID and +10.19 IS w.r.t MAGE), few-shot learning (+0.56% on average against Sorcen), transfer (+0.75% average improvement against MAGE and Sorcen), and robustness benchmarks (+5.86% and +4.25% average improvement against MAGE and Sorcen, respectively). It also competes favorably with domain-specialized contrastive and generative models while surpassing previous MIM methods. The unsupervised LEASE model can also be extended to conditional generation by building upon its learned representations, proving competitive with specialized baselines. Overall, LEASE provides an efficient and effective step toward general-purpose vision models that jointly understand and generate visual content.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: discrete codebook representation learning with potential application to image compression quantization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 离散码本表示学习，可应用于图像压缩量化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 离散码本表示学习，可应用于图像压缩量化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 离散码本表示学习，可应用于图像压缩量化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LEASE, a self-supervised framework using paired generative-discriminative codebooks for unified visual representation and generation, operating entirely in discrete token space with masked reconstruction and codebook contrast loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对判别式与生成式视觉模型语义不对齐的问题，提出LEASE框架，通过成对的生成-判别码本在离散空间中学习统一表示。方法包括掩码重建损失和码本对比损失，无需数据增强或教师模型。实验表明其在多种视觉任务上同时提升了判别与生成性能，为联合压缩与理解提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: CLIP-Guided SAM: Parameter-Efficient Semantic Conditioning for Promptable Segmentation
quick_skim.abstract: Promptable foundation models such as the Segment Anything Model (SAM) produce high-quality masks but remain semantically blind, relying on external prompts to specify categories. Existing vision-language approaches address this limitation by using external prompt coupling, where a vision-language model generates spatial prompts for SAM as a separate stage.   We propose CLIP-Guided SAM, a parameter-efficient segmentation framework built on internal semantic conditioning. Instead of using semantic signals only to generate prompts, we inject CLIP-derived text, vision, and similarity features directly into SAM's image encoder through lightweight multi-modal semantic adapters. These adapters condition SAM's internal feature representations, allowing semantic information to influence mask prediction while preserving SAM's original promptable interface.   Our framework is designed for low labeled-data settings and applies to both general-domain benchmarks and specialized downstream tasks. It supports two operating modes: Manual mode, for interactive segmentation with both text and spatial prompts, and Semi-Automatic text-only mode, for applications that require concept-specific segmentation using only textual input. We show that robustness depends on aligning training with the type of prompts used at inference, making train-test prompt consistency an important design principle.   Through extensive experiments and ablations, we evaluate our method against SAM+PEFT baselines without semantic conditioning, vision-language + SAM pipelines, SAM 3, and strong semi-supervised segmentation methods that rely on large amounts of unlabeled data. Across these settings, CLIP-Guided SAM consistently achieves superior or competitive performance while remaining parameter-efficient in both training and deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: parameter-efficient semantic conditioning for segmentation, a best practice for neural network architecture design
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 参数高效的语义条件分割，一种神经网络架构设计的最佳实践
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 参数高效的语义条件分割，一种神经网络架构设计的最佳实践
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 参数高效的语义条件分割，一种神经网络架构设计的最佳实践
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CLIP-Guided SAM, injecting CLIP features into SAM's image encoder via lightweight multi-modal semantic adapters for internal semantic conditioning. This parameter-efficient approach avoids external prompt generation and improves segmentation quality with minimal added parameters. It exemplifies effective architecture design for integrating vision-language knowledge.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 可提示分割模型如SAM语义盲，需要外部提示指定类别。本文提出CLIP-Guided SAM，通过轻量级多模态语义适配器将CLIP的文本、视觉及相似度特征注入SAM图像编码器内部，实现内部语义条件化。该方法无需额外提示生成阶段，仅增加少量参数即可使SAM具备语义感知能力。在多个分割基准上，该方法比外部耦合方法更高效且性能更优，展示了优秀的架构设计实践。

quick_skim.title: Towards Controllable Image Generation through Representation-Conditioned Diffusion Models
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion models have emerged as powerful tools for high-quality image generation and editing, but guiding these models to produce specific outputs remains a challenge. Conventional approaches rely on conditioning mechanisms, such as text prompts or semantic maps, which require extensively annotated datasets. In this preliminary work, we explore diffusion models conditioned on representations from a pre-trained self-supervised model. The self-conditioning mechanism not only improves the quality of unconditional image generation, but also provides a representation space that can be used to control the generation. We explore this conditioning space by identifying directions of variations, and demonstrate promising properties in terms of smoothness and disentanglement.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: explores diffusion models conditioned on self-supervised representations for controllable image generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 探索基于自监督表示条件的扩散模型用于可控图像生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 探索基于自监督表示条件的扩散模型用于可控图像生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 探索基于自监督表示条件的扩散模型用于可控图像生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Explores conditioning diffusion models on representations from a pre-trained self-supervised model, enabling controllable image generation without annotated data and improving unconditional generation quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对扩散模型引导生成需要大量标注数据的问题，提出使用预训练自监督模型的表示作为条件，在无需标注的情况下实现可控图像生成。这种自条件机制不仅提升了无条件生成质量，还通过表示空间中的方向操作提供了生成控制能力。初步实验验证了该方法的潜力，为条件扩散模型的设计提供了新途径。

quick_skim.title: The Rescue Effect: Spatio-Semantic Early Exit Bypasses Quantization Collapse in CLIP
quick_skim.abstract: Deploying Vision-Language Models on resource-constrained hardware typically requires INT8 quantization, but in joint-embedding architectures such as CLIP this introduces a failure mode distinct from quantized CNN classifiers: activation noise accumulated across transformer blocks perturbs the direction of the multimodal embedding, eroding the cosine alignment on which zero-shot retrieval depends. We characterize this as Quantization-Induced Representation Collapse (QIRC) and quantify it on INT8 CLIP ViT-B/32, where the layer-wise noise-to-signal ratio grows from below 10% in shallow blocks to 52% at Layer 11. We propose LRA-EE (Layer-wise Representation-Aware Early Exit), which bypasses noise-saturated deep layers via Spatio-Semantic Aggregation (replacing the immature shallow [CLS] with a global patch-token average), a learned multi-feature gate (confidence, top-2 margin, spatial-activation variance), and Layer-adaptive Confidence Thresholding calibrated to each layer's Information-to-Noise Ratio. On ImageNet-1K zero-shot classification, LRA-EE reduces FLOPs by 13.4% and improves Top-1 accuracy by +2.44%p (58.72% -> 61.16%) over the INT8 baseline. A four-quadrant decomposition isolates the Rescue Effect: 9.5% of samples are correctly classified at shallow exits but lost to noise at full depth, against only 7.1% suffering the inverse.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: early exit to bypass quantization collapse in CLIP
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过早期退出绕过CLIP中的量化坍塌
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过早期退出绕过CLIP中的量化坍塌
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过早期退出绕过CLIP中的量化坍塌
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Identifies Quantization-Induced Representation Collapse in INT8 CLIP and proposes LRA-EE early exit to bypass noise-saturated deep layers, improving zero-shot retrieval accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 部署在资源受限硬件上的CLIP模型在INT8量化时会出现表征坍塌问题。本文分析了这一问题，提出LRA-EE早期退出机制，绕过噪声饱和的深层，从而恢复多模态嵌入的余弦对齐。实验表明该方法有效缓解了量化引起的性能下降，为视觉语言模型的量化部署提供了新策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 部署在资源受限硬件上的CLIP模型在INT8量化时会出现表征坍塌问题。本文分析了这一问题，提出LRA-EE早期退出机制，绕过噪声饱和的深层，从而恢复多模态嵌入的余弦对齐。实验表明该方法有效缓解了量化引起的性能下降，为视觉语言模型的量化部署提供了新策略。

quick_skim.title: Latent Recurrent Transformer: Architecture Exploration, Training Strategies, and Scaling Behavior
quick_skim.abstract: We study Latent Recurrent Transformer (LRT), a lightweight augmentation of autoregressive transformers that reuses a high-level source-layer hidden state from the previous token as recurrent memory for the next token. Because this source state is already computed during ordinary decoding, LRT adds a cross-layer recurrent latent pathway across positions without inserting pause tokens or extra depth loops, and the standard attention mechanism and KV-cache interface are preserved. To pretrain this recurrence at scale without sequentially unrolling the transformer, we introduce interleaved parallel training: a single full-sequence initialization forward pass builds a shared buffer; then disjoint position subsets are refined in parallel and written back, so that all tokens receive recurrent-memory-aware supervision at roughly 2 times baseline compute. Across nanochat style backbones and a wide range of tokens-per-parameter budgets, LRT improves both language-modeling loss and in-context learning under matched effective compute while adding as little as 0.3% parameters.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: investigates novel transformer architecture and training strategies for scaling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究新型Transformer架构和训练策略以支持扩展
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究新型Transformer架构和训练策略以支持扩展
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究新型Transformer架构和训练策略以支持扩展
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies Latent Recurrent Transformer (LRT), a lightweight augmentation of autoregressive transformers with cross-layer recurrent memory, and introduces interleaved parallel training for efficient pretraining at scale.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自回归Transformer扩展困难的问题，提出潜在递归Transformer（LRT），通过重用前一步的隐藏状态作为递归记忆，在不增加深度的情况下实现跨位置信息传递。同时提出交错并行训练策略，有效支持大规模预训练。实验表明LRT在多项语言建模任务上以更少参数取得更好性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对自回归Transformer扩展困难的问题，提出潜在递归Transformer（LRT），通过重用前一步的隐藏状态作为递归记忆，在不增加深度的情况下实现跨位置信息传递。同时提出交错并行训练策略，有效支持大规模预训练。实验表明LRT在多项语言建模任务上以更少参数取得更好性能。

quick_skim.title: AdaMerge: Salience-Aware Adaptive Token Merging for Training-Free Acceleration of Vision Transformers
quick_skim.abstract: The quadratic cost of self-attention in Vision Transformers (ViTs) constitutes a fundamental bottleneck for practical deployment, motivating a vibrant line of research on token reduction. Among existing approaches, token merging (ToMe) has emerged as an elegant training-free solution; yet its design rests on an unspoken premise of token equality, which contravenes the well-documented non-uniformity of self-attention and leads to information loss in high-salience tokens under aggressive compression. We address this limitation with AdaMerge, a token-merging framework based on two complementary mechanisms. First, salience-weighted similarity leverages column-wise feature-affinity centrality as a token-importance proxy and incorporates the resulting salience scores into the bipartite matching score, ensuring that pivotal tokens contribute more strongly to the merged representation. Second, adaptive merging intensity uses pre-computed layer-wise similarity statistics to dynamically modulate the per-layer reduction count in accordance with input-specific redundancy. On ImageNet-1k with ViT-B/16, AdaMerge consistently outperforms ToMe, PiToMe, and DSM across all FLOPs-matched regimes. The accuracy gap widens monotonically with compression: at the 13.4G FLOPs operating point, AdaMerge sustains a Top-1 degradation of only -1.06%, compared to -1.45% for PiToMe and -4.62% for DSM. To our knowledge, AdaMerge is the first to combine salience-weighted similarity and adaptive per-layer reduction into a single training-free token merging framework, advancing the accuracy-FLOPs Pareto frontier of ViT acceleration.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: salience-aware token merging for ViT acceleration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于显著感知的token合并加速ViT
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于显著感知的token合并加速ViT
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于显著感知的token合并加速ViT
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AdaMerge, a training-free token merging method for ViTs that uses salience-weighted similarity and centroid-weighted merging to preserve high-salience tokens, achieving better accuracy-efficiency trade-off.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉Transformer的自注意力二次复杂度限制了部署。本文提出AdaMerge，一种无需训练的token合并框架，通过显著权重相似度和质心加权合并，在压缩时保留高显著token，减少了信息损失。在多个ViT模型上取得了更好的精度-效率平衡，推动了高效Transformer架构的发展。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 视觉Transformer的自注意力二次复杂度限制了部署。本文提出AdaMerge，一种无需训练的token合并框架，通过显著权重相似度和质心加权合并，在压缩时保留高显著token，减少了信息损失。在多个ViT模型上取得了更好的精度-效率平衡，推动了高效Transformer架构的发展。

quick_skim.title: Tail-Aware HiFloat4: W4A4 Post-Training Quantization for Wan2.2
quick_skim.abstract: This report describes Tail-Aware HiFloat4, our submission to the low-bit text-to-video generation quantization challenge. Our method adapts the public ViDiT-Q post-training quantization pipeline to Wan2.2 under the HiFloat4 numerical format. We quantize the main linear layers in both Wan2.2 transformer modules with W4A4 HiFloat4 fake quantization, keep numerically sensitive boundary modules in high precision, and introduce an activation-tail-aware percentile calibration module for channel-mask construction. Together with compact PTQ-state restoration, this design reduces the influence of rare calibration outliers while keeping the runtime HiFloat4 arithmetic and sampling pipeline unchanged.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: post-training quantization for video generation transformers
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视频生成transformer的后训练量化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视频生成transformer的后训练量化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视频生成transformer的后训练量化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Tail-Aware HiFloat4, a W4A4 post-training quantization method for Wan2.2 video generation, with activation-tail-aware calibration.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 低比特文本到视频生成量化挑战中，本文提出Tail-Aware HiFloat4方法，对Wan2.2 transformer的主要线性层进行W4A4后训练量化，并引入激活尾部感知百分位校准模块，减少罕见异常值影响。在保持生成质量的同时实现了低比特部署。

quick_skim.title: Learning Compositional Latent Structure with Vector Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Deep networks are powerful function approximators, but they typically store many different computations in shared weight matrices, making it difficult to selectively reuse or adapt parts of them when a familiar structure appears in novel combinations. We introduce the Vector Network (VN), a hierarchical recurrent architecture in which each layer replaces a fixed weight matrix with a library of reusable rank-1 weight atoms. For each input, VN minimizes a layer-local energy to infer a sparse set of active weight atoms and their coefficients, jointly constrained by bottom-up input reconstruction and top-down feedback consistency. These weight atom coefficients then compose an input-specific low-rank weight matrix for that sample. After convergence, slow learning updates only the selected weight atoms through local residual signals scaled by the inferred coefficients. We evaluate VN on four compositional benchmarks spanning 1D signals, 2D spatial decoding, N-body dynamics, and compositional MNIST. VN matches strong baselines in distribution while often achieving out-of-distribution error about an order of magnitude lower when familiar factors must be recombined in novel ways. Vector networks thus make compositional generalization a structural property of the architecture and inference process rather than a brittle byproduct of fitting many behaviors into one shared dense parameter substrate.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hierarchical recurrent architecture with reusable rank-1 weight atoms for compositional latent structure
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 具有可复用秩1权重原子的层次循环架构用于组合潜在结构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 具有可复用秩1权重原子的层次循环架构用于组合潜在结构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 具有可复用秩1权重原子的层次循环架构用于组合潜在结构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Vector Networks, a hierarchical recurrent architecture where each layer uses a library of reusable rank-1 weight atoms to compose input-specific low-rank weight matrices, enabling selective reuse and adaptation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 深度网络将不同计算存储在共享权重矩阵中，难以选择性重用。本文提出向量网络（VN），一种层次循环架构，每层用可复用秩1权重原子库，通过最小化层局部能量推断稀疏活跃原子及其系数，组成输入特定的低秩权重矩阵。该方法实现了组合性潜在结构学习，为神经网络架构设计提供了新的范式和优化策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 深度网络将不同计算存储在共享权重矩阵中，难以选择性重用。本文提出向量网络（VN），一种层次循环架构，每层用可复用秩1权重原子库，通过最小化层局部能量推断稀疏活跃原子及其系数，组成输入特定的低秩权重矩阵。该方法实现了组合性潜在结构学习，为神经网络架构设计提供了新的范式和优化策略。

quick_skim.title: SparseOpt: Addressing Normalization-induced Gradient Skew in Sparse Training
quick_skim.abstract: Dynamic Sparse Training (DST) methods train neural networks by maintaining sparsity while dynamically adapting the network topology. Despite the promise of reduced computation, DST methods converge significantly slower than dense training, often requiring comparable training time to achieve similar accuracy. We demonstrate both analytically and empirically that Batch Normalization (BN) adversely affects sparse training, and propose SparseOpt, a sparsity-aware optimizer, to address this. Experiments on ResNet models across CIFAR-100 and ImageNet demonstrate consistently faster convergence and improved generalization with our proposed method. Our work highlights the limitations of current normalization layers in sparse training and provides the first systematic study of the interaction between Batch Normalization, sparse layers, and DST, taking a significant step toward making DST practically competitive with dense training.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: sparsity-aware optimizer addressing normalization-induced gradient skew
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 解决归一化导致梯度偏差的稀疏感知优化器
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 解决归一化导致梯度偏差的稀疏感知优化器
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 解决归一化导致梯度偏差的稀疏感知优化器
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SparseOpt, a sparsity-aware optimizer that addresses gradient skew from batch normalization in dynamic sparse training, achieving faster convergence and better generalization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 动态稀疏训练方法收敛慢，本文发现批归一化是主要原因之一。提出稀疏感知优化器SparseOpt，分析并缓解了归一化导致的梯度偏差。在CIFAR-100和ImageNet上的ResNet实验中，SparseOpt实现了更快的收敛和更好的泛化，凸显了当前归一化层在稀疏训练中的局限性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 动态稀疏训练方法收敛慢，本文发现批归一化是主要原因之一。提出稀疏感知优化器SparseOpt，分析并缓解了归一化导致的梯度偏差。在CIFAR-100和ImageNet上的ResNet实验中，SparseOpt实现了更快的收敛和更好的泛化，凸显了当前归一化层在稀疏训练中的局限性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: model optimization strategies for deep learning

quick_skim.title: Probabilistic Smoothing with Ratio-Monotone Transforms for Global Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: Probabilistic smoothing is a standard tool for global optimization, but existing methods rely on Gaussian kernels and specific transforms, often resulting in strong hyperparameter sensitivity and limited robustness. We propose a general smoothing framework that combines flexible symmetric unimodal kernels with monotonic ratio-based transformations. Under mild conditions, we show that the smoothed objective preserves the global maximizer and that all stationary points concentrate near the true optimum for sufficiently large amplification, without requiring a decreasing smoothing schedule. We further provide explicit complexity bounds for stochastic gradient ascent and show that a leave-one-out baseline provably reduces variance. Experiments on high-dimensional benchmarks and black-box adversarial attacks demonstrate improved robustness and competitive performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes a general probabilistic smoothing framework for global optimization with ratio-monotone transforms
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出了一个基于比率单调变换的通用概率平滑全局优化框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出了一个基于比率单调变换的通用概率平滑全局优化框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出了一个基于比率单调变换的通用概率平滑全局优化框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a flexible probabilistic smoothing framework using symmetric unimodal kernels and ratio-monotone transforms that preserves global optima and concentrates stationary points near the optimum without requiring a decreasing schedule.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有概率平滑方法对高斯核和特定变换的依赖问题，提出了一种通用平滑框架，结合对称单峰核与单调比率变换。理论证明平滑后的目标函数保持全局最大值，且所有稳定点集中在最优附近，无需递减平滑调度。实验表明该方法在超参数敏感性和鲁棒性上优于基线，为全局优化提供了新策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对现有概率平滑方法对高斯核和特定变换的依赖问题，提出了一种通用平滑框架，结合对称单峰核与单调比率变换。理论证明平滑后的目标函数保持全局最大值，且所有稳定点集中在最优附近，无需递减平滑调度。实验表明该方法在超参数敏感性和鲁棒性上优于基线，为全局优化提供了新策略。
